name: dev-workflow
description: Developer-workflow skills — git memory, decision briefs, critique, recap, handoff, session distill.

interface.shortDescription: Process skills for the work around the code
interface.longDescription: Skills for the engineering work that surrounds writing code — git-memory (record why a decision was made into commit/PR memory trailers, then recall it later), brief-before-asking (a 6-block briefing before a hard fork question), proposal-critique and complexity-critique (triage a plan or a single change by a deletion-first, YAGNI lens), recap-state and handoff (re-orient mid-session or save state for the next session), distill-sessions (mine past transcripts for friction patterns), and dbt-model-style (a dbt + Redshift model style contract). Use them to decide well, remember why, and hand off cleanly. Bundles a PostToolUse hook that validates skill folder structure on edit.
defaultPrompt: Before I commit to this approach, give me a briefing on the trade-offs.
Critique this plan — what can we drop or defer?
Record why we chose this design into git memory so we can recall it later.
